# NodeLabz — Phase 1 Build Plan (Marketing Layer)
# ⚠️ DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE — This is the locked Phase 1 execution plan approved by the founder.
# Any changes must be discussed before modifying this document.

**Date locked:** March 16, 2026
**Approved by:** Federico Tafur
**Status:** LOCKED — Execution plan
**Target:** 12-week sprint cycle
**Goal:** Fully functional AI marketing data platform with real integrations, ready for first 50 paying LATAM clients

---

## Current State: ~25% Complete

### What's Built (Solid)
- ✅ Dashboard shell, navigation, multi-tenant architecture
- ✅ Supabase Auth (Google/GitHub OAuth, auto-provisioning, 4 system roles)
- ✅ Prisma schema (22 models — Contact, Deal, Pipeline, Integration, Workflow, EmailTemplate, CampaignMetric, HealthScore, etc.)
- ✅ 4 tRPC routers (auth, superadmin, workflow, conversation)
- ✅ AI chat panel (SSE streaming to external AI service, conversation persistence)
- ✅ Workflow editor (@xyflow/react — full drag-and-drop, 4 node types, CRUD)
- ✅ Super admin flow (Gestion de Clientes, company switching, tenant management)
- ✅ Visualization wireframes wired into sidebar:
  - Node Map (@xyflow/react) — marketing ecosystem graph
  - Metricas (Recharts) — KPI sparklines, 4 chart types
  - Pipeline Builder (@xyflow/react) — funnel with conversion rates
  - Email Builder (@dnd-kit) — drag-and-drop block composer
  - Form Builder (@dnd-kit) — drag fields, conditional logic
  - Report Builder (Recharts) — funnel, treemap, composed charts
  - Network Graph (Cytoscape.js) — full interaction network demo

### What's a Stub (skeleton exists, no real logic)
- ⬜ `sync-integration` worker — has `TODO` comment, no platform logic
- ⬜ `generate-health-score` worker — has 4 `TODO` comments
- ⬜ `send-email` worker — has `TODO: Send via Amazon SES`
- ⬜ Billing page — UI shows plan info, no Stripe SDK/webhooks
- ⬜ Integration model — stores credentials shape, no OAuth flows

### What's Missing (not built at all)
- ❌ CRM routers (contacts, deals, pipeline, activities)
- ❌ Campaign routers (campaigns, email campaigns)
- ❌ Meta Ads API integration
- ❌ Google Ads API integration
- ❌ GA4 API integration
- ❌ TikTok Ads API integration
- ❌ WhatsApp Cloud API integration
- ❌ Stripe billing (SDK, webhooks, subscriptions)
- ❌ Email sending (SES/Resend)
- ❌ Health score calculation algorithm
- ❌ Integration OAuth flows (platform-specific, not user login)
- ❌ PDF report generation
- ❌ Plan enforcement / feature gates
- ❌ Contact import (CSV)
- ❌ Real AI insights (AI connected to actual client data)
- ❌ Notification system
- ❌ Onboarding wizard

## Sprint Plan

### Sprint 1 — CRM Foundation + Billing (Weeks 1-2)

**Goal:** Users can manage contacts, deals, and pipeline. Stripe billing works.

| # | Task | Details | Files |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 1.1 | **Contacts tRPC router** | CRUD for contacts: list (paginated, searchable), create, update, delete, bulk import. Use existing `Contact` Prisma model. Tenant-scoped. | `server/trpc/routers/contacts.ts` |
| 1.2 | **Deals tRPC router** | CRUD for deals: list by pipeline stage, create, update (move between stages), delete. Link to contacts. Use existing `Deal` model. | `server/trpc/routers/deals.ts` |
| 1.3 | **Pipeline tRPC router** | CRUD for pipeline stages: list, create, reorder, update, delete. Default pipeline created at tenant provisioning (already done). | `server/trpc/routers/pipeline.ts` |
| 1.4 | **Activities tRPC router** | Log activities: calls, emails, meetings, notes. Link to contacts/deals. Use existing `Activity` model. | `server/trpc/routers/activities.ts` |
| 1.5 | **Stripe billing integration** | Install `stripe` SDK. Create checkout sessions, webhook handler (`/api/webhooks/stripe`), subscription management, plan upgrades/downgrades. Update `stripeCustomerId` on Tenant. | `server/stripe/`, `app/api/webhooks/stripe/` |
| 1.6 | **Plan enforcement middleware** | tRPC middleware that checks tenant plan before allowing gated features (workflow save, AI write mode, etc.). Reference plan tiers from PRODUCT-VISION.md. | `server/trpc/middleware/plan-gate.ts` |
| 1.7 | **Wire CRM pages to real data** | Replace mock data in Contacts, Pipeline, Deals sidebar pages with real tRPC queries. | `components/pages/contacts-pages.tsx` |

**Deliverable:** Working CRM + payment flow. User can add contacts, manage deals, pay for a plan.

---

### Sprint 2 — Meta Ads Integration + Data Sync (Weeks 3-4)

**Goal:** Users can connect their Meta Ads account and see real campaign data.

| # | Task | Details | Files |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 2.1 | **Integration OAuth framework** | Generic OAuth flow handler: redirect to provider → callback → store tokens in `Integration` model. Support token refresh. | `server/integrations/oauth.ts`, `app/api/integrations/[provider]/` |
| 2.2 | **Meta Ads OAuth** | Implement Meta OAuth flow (Facebook Login for Business). Request `ads_read`, `ads_management`, `pages_read_engagement` permissions. Store access token + page tokens. | `server/integrations/meta/auth.ts` |
| 2.3 | **Meta Ads sync worker** | Trigger.dev task: pull campaigns, ad sets, ads, spend, impressions, clicks, conversions from Meta Marketing API. Store in `CampaignMetric` model. Run on schedule (every 6h). | `workers/src/triggers/sync-meta.ts` |
| 2.4 | **Meta CAPI (Conversions API)** | Server-side event tracking: send purchase, lead, and custom events back to Meta for attribution. | `server/integrations/meta/capi.ts` |
| 2.5 | **Integrations tRPC router** | CRUD for integrations: list connected, connect new (trigger OAuth), disconnect, check sync status, force re-sync. | `server/trpc/routers/integrations.ts` |
| 2.6 | **Wire Integrations page** | Replace mock integration cards with real connection status. "Conectar" triggers OAuth. Show last sync time, status badge. | `components/pages/remaining-pages.tsx` |
| 2.7 | **Wire Campaign pages** | Replace mock campaign data with real Meta Ads metrics from DB. Show real spend, leads, ROAS per campaign. | `components/pages/campaigns-pages.tsx` |

**Deliverable:** User connects Meta Ads → sees real campaign data in dashboard. CAPI sends conversion events back.

---

### Sprint 3 — Google Ads + GA4 + Health Score (Weeks 5-6)

**Goal:** Google ecosystem connected. Health Score calculates from real data.

| # | Task | Details | Files |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 3.1 | **Google Ads OAuth** | Google OAuth with `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords` scope. Store refresh token. | `server/integrations/google/auth.ts` |
| 3.2 | **Google Ads sync worker** | Pull campaigns, ad groups, keywords, spend, clicks, conversions from Google Ads API. Store in `CampaignMetric`. Schedule every 6h. | `workers/src/triggers/sync-google-ads.ts` |
| 3.3 | **GA4 OAuth + sync** | Google OAuth with analytics scope. Pull sessions, users, conversions, traffic sources from GA4 Data API. Store metrics. | `server/integrations/google/ga4.ts`, `workers/src/triggers/sync-ga4.ts` |
| 3.4 | **Health Score algorithm** | Calculate 5-pillar score (0-100): Marketing (campaign performance), CRM (pipeline health), Automations (active workflows), Integrations (connected count + sync status), Data Quality (completeness, freshness). Store in `HealthScore` model. | `workers/src/triggers/generate-health-score.ts` |
| 3.5 | **Health Score tRPC** | Router to fetch current + historical health scores. Trend data for sparklines. | `server/trpc/routers/health-score.ts` |
| 3.6 | **Wire Health Score page** | Replace placeholder with real Tremor radial gauge + Recharts radar chart using real pillar scores. | `components/pages/dashboard-pages.tsx` |
| 3.7 | **Wire Node Map to real data** | Node Map shows actual connected integrations (from Integration model) with real sync status (healthy/warning/broken). | `components/pages/dashboard-pages.tsx` |

**Deliverable:** Google Ads + GA4 connected. Health Score shows real 5-pillar analysis. Node Map reflects actual ecosystem.

---

### Sprint 4 — Email Engine + Campaign Management (Weeks 7-8)

**Goal:** Users can send real emails, create campaigns, run sequences.

| # | Task | Details | Files |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 4.1 | **Email service (Resend)** | Install `resend` SDK. Service wrapper: send transactional emails, send bulk campaigns, handle bounces/complaints. | `server/email/resend.ts` |
| 4.2 | **Send email worker** | Implement `send-email` Trigger.dev task. Template rendering (React Email), personalization with merge tags ({{nombre}}, {{empresa}}). Batch sending for campaigns. | `workers/src/triggers/send-email.ts` |
| 4.3 | **Email campaigns tRPC** | CRUD for email campaigns: create (subject, template, audience), schedule, send, pause. Track delivery, opens, clicks via webhooks. | `server/trpc/routers/email-campaigns.ts` |
| 4.4 | **Email templates tRPC** | CRUD for email templates. Store the block-based structure from Email Builder. Render to HTML for sending. | `server/trpc/routers/email-templates.ts` |
| 4.5 | **Email webhook handler** | Handle Resend webhooks: delivery, open, click, bounce, complaint. Update `EmailCampaign` metrics. | `app/api/webhooks/resend/route.ts` |
| 4.6 | **Drip sequences** | Sequence model: ordered list of emails with delays. Trigger.dev scheduled tasks to send next email in sequence per contact. | `server/trpc/routers/sequences.ts`, `workers/src/triggers/drip-sequence.ts` |
| 4.7 | **Wire Email Builder to save** | Email Builder saves block structure to DB via templates router. "Preview" renders to HTML. "Send" creates campaign. | `components/pages/email-pages.tsx` |

**Deliverable:** Real email sending works. Users create templates in builder, send campaigns, track opens/clicks.

---

### Sprint 5 — WhatsApp + Contact Import + OAuth Flows (Weeks 9-10)

**Goal:** WhatsApp two-way messaging. CSV contact import. All OAuth flows polished.

| # | Task | Details | Files |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 5.1 | **WhatsApp Cloud API integration** | Register webhook with Meta. Handle incoming messages. Send template messages + free-form replies. | `server/integrations/whatsapp/`, `app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` |
| 5.2 | **WhatsApp tRPC router** | List conversations, send message, manage templates, broadcast to segments. | `server/trpc/routers/whatsapp.ts` |
| 5.3 | **WhatsApp auto-responses** | Rule-based auto-replies: keyword triggers, business hours replies, FAQ bot. | `server/integrations/whatsapp/auto-reply.ts` |
| 5.4 | **Contact import (CSV)** | Upload CSV → field mapping UI → preview → import with deduplication (match by email/phone). Bulk create via `contacts` router. | `components/ui/contact-import-wizard.tsx` |
| 5.5 | **TikTok Ads integration** | OAuth + sync worker. Same pattern as Meta/Google. Pull campaign metrics. | `server/integrations/tiktok/`, `workers/src/triggers/sync-tiktok.ts` |
| 5.6 | **Shopify integration** | OAuth + sync worker. Pull orders, products, customers, revenue. | `server/integrations/shopify/`, `workers/src/triggers/sync-shopify.ts` |
| 5.7 | **Wire WhatsApp pages** | Replace placeholder WhatsApp pages with real conversation UI + template management. | `components/pages/remaining-pages.tsx` |

**Deliverable:** WhatsApp messaging works. Users import contacts via CSV. TikTok + Shopify connected.

---

### Sprint 6 — AI Intelligence + Reports + Polish (Weeks 11-12)

**Goal:** AI gives real insights from real data. PDF reports. Launch-ready polish.

| # | Task | Details | Files |
|---|------|---------|-------|
| 6.1 | **Connect AI to real data** | AI service queries actual tenant data (campaign metrics, contacts, deals, health score) to generate real insights. Not mock text. | `nodelabz-ai/` service updates |
| 6.2 | **AI daily digest** | Trigger.dev scheduled task: every morning, AI analyzes last 24h of data, generates digest, stores as notification + optional email. | `workers/src/triggers/ai-digest.ts` |
| 6.3 | **AI recommendations engine** | AI analyzes campaign performance, identifies anomalies, generates actionable recommendations (pause campaign, reallocate budget, etc.). | `nodelabz-ai/` + tRPC router |
| 6.4 | **PDF report generation** | Install `@react-pdf/renderer`. Generate white-labeled PDF reports from real data. Export from Report Builder. | `server/reports/pdf-generator.ts` |
| 6.5 | **Scheduled reports** | Trigger.dev cron: daily/weekly/monthly report generation + email delivery to configured recipients. | `workers/src/triggers/scheduled-report.ts` |
| 6.6 | **Notification system** | In-app notifications: new lead, integration broken, health score dropped, AI alert. Bell icon in navbar with dropdown. | `server/trpc/routers/notifications.ts`, `components/ui/notifications.tsx` |
| 6.7 | **Onboarding wizard** | First-login flow: connect your first integration → import contacts → set up pipeline → create first automation. Progress tracker. | `components/ui/onboarding-wizard.tsx` |
| 6.8 | **Launch polish** | Error boundaries, loading states, empty states, mobile responsiveness check, performance audit, security audit (rate limiting, input validation). | Various |

**Deliverable:** Platform is launch-ready. AI gives real insights. Reports export to PDF. New users guided through onboarding.

| Dependency | When Needed | Status |

| Meta Developer App | Sprint 2 | Need to create app, get app review |
| Google Cloud Project | Sprint 3 | Need OAuth consent screen, API enablement |
| TikTok Developer Account | Sprint 5 | Need to register for Marketing API |
| Resend account | Sprint 4 | Need API key, domain verification |
| Stripe account | Sprint 1 | Need product/price IDs, webhook endpoint |
| WhatsApp Business Account | Sprint 5 | Need Meta Business verification, phone number |
| Shopify Partner Account | Sprint 5 | Need app credentials |
| AWS SES (fallback for bulk) | Sprint 4 | Optional, Resend handles both |
| AI Service (nodelabz-ai) | Sprint 6 | Needs to exist and accept tenant-scoped queries |

## Success Criteria — Phase 1 Complete When:

1. ✅ User signs up → onboarding wizard guides setup
2. ✅ User connects Meta Ads → sees real campaign data within 1 hour
3. ✅ User connects Google Ads + GA4 → sees real performance data
4. ✅ User adds contacts (manual + CSV import) → CRM pipeline works
5. ✅ User creates email template in builder → sends real campaign → sees opens/clicks
6. ✅ User connects WhatsApp → sends/receives real messages
7. ✅ Health Score calculates from real data → shows 5-pillar breakdown
8. ✅ Node Map shows actual connected integrations with real sync status
9. ✅ AI chat gives insights based on actual client data (not mock)
10. ✅ PDF report exports with real data
11. ✅ Stripe billing works → user can upgrade plan → features gate correctly
12. ✅ Workflow editor saves workflows → basic execution (send email, update deal stage)
13. ✅ 50 beta clients onboarded in Costa Rica

---

## Risk Mitigation

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| Meta API review takes weeks | Apply in Sprint 1, start development with test account |
| Google Ads API requires MCC | Set up MCC early, use test accounts for development |
| AI service not ready for Sprint 6 | Use Bedrock directly as provider, refactor to dedicated service later |
| AI costs spike unexpectedly | Semantic caching + model routing + per-tenant budgets + CloudWatch alarms |
| Higgsfield API instability (new API) | Segmind as fallback (same models, different provider), or Bedrock Stable Diffusion for images |
| Stripe webhook complexity | Use Stripe CLI for local testing, extensive webhook logging |
| Email deliverability issues | Start with Resend (handles reputation), monitor bounce rates from day 1 |
| WhatsApp Business verification slow | Apply in Sprint 3 (2 sprints before needed), use sandbox meanwhile |

---

## AI Infrastructure — AWS Bedrock + Cost Minimization

### Why Bedrock

AWS Bedrock gives us access to Claude (Anthropic), Llama (Meta), Mistral, Titan (Amazon), and Stable Diffusion through **one unified API** — no separate API keys per provider, volume discounts, and provisioned throughput for predictable costs.

```
User Request
     │
     v
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│  AI Router (Intent Classifier)  │  ← Classifies complexity + intent
└──────────┬──────────────────────┘
           │
     ┌─────┴──────┬──────────────┐
     v            v              v
  TIER 1       TIER 2         TIER 3
  (cheap)      (mid)          (premium)
┌──────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌──────────────┐
│ Haiku    │ │ Sonnet    │ │ Opus         │
│ $0.25/M  │ │ $3/M in   │ │ $15/M in     │
│ in tokens│ │ tokens    │ │ tokens       │
└──────────┘ └───────────┘ └──────────────┘
```

### Model Routing Strategy — Minimize Cost Per Request

Every AI request gets classified by an ultra-cheap classifier (Haiku or rule-based) before being routed:

| Request Type | Route To | Cost | Examples |
|-------------|----------|------|----------|
| **Formatting / Lookups** | Haiku ($0.25/1M input) | ~$0.001 | "Cuantos leads tengo?", "Formato de fecha", field validation |
| **Summaries / Basic Analysis** | Sonnet ($3/1M input) | ~$0.01 | "Resume mis metricas de la semana", "Compara Meta vs Google", daily digests |
| **Strategy / Cross-Domain / Generation** | Opus ($15/1M input) | ~$0.05 | "Por que bajaron mis leads?", workflow generation, report insights, campaign recommendations |
| **Image Generation** | Stable Diffusion (Bedrock) or Higgsfield | ~$0.05-0.12 | Ad creatives, email banners, social media images |
| **Video Generation** | Higgsfield API | ~$0.86 | Video ads, product demos, social reels |

### 7 Cost Minimization Strategies

**1. Semantic Caching (saves 30-50% of AI costs)**
Cache AI responses by semantic similarity. If a user asks "how are my leads doing?" and another asks "whats my lead count?" — same answer. Use pgvector embeddings to match similar queries.

```
User query → embed with Titan Embeddings ($0.0001) → search cache →
  if match (similarity > 0.92): return cached response ($0)
  if no match: route to LLM → cache response for future
```

**2. Prompt Compression (saves 20-30% per request)**
Strip unnecessary context from prompts. Only include relevant tenant data, not the entire schema. Use structured data summaries instead of raw dumps.

```
BAD:  "Here are all 1,247 contacts with full details..." (50K tokens)
GOOD: "Summary: 1,247 contacts, 42 closed deals, $45K revenue, top channel: Meta Ads" (200 tokens)
```

**3. Batch Processing for Non-Urgent Tasks**
Daily digests, health score insights, report generation, campaign recommendations — batch these into scheduled Trigger.dev jobs that run during off-peak hours (cheaper with Bedrock provisioned throughput).

**4. Response Streaming with Early Termination**
Stream responses. If the user gets their answer in the first 100 tokens, stop generating. Don't waste tokens on verbose explanations nobody reads.

**5. Tiered Model Access by Plan**
Different plans get different AI quality — this is already in the autonomy table:

| Plan | AI Model Access | Monthly AI Budget |
|------|----------------|-------------------|
| INICIO | Haiku only (read-only insights) | ~$2/tenant |
| CRECIMIENTO | Haiku + Sonnet | ~$8/tenant |
| PROFESIONAL | Haiku + Sonnet + Opus | ~$15/tenant |
| AGENCIA | Full access + image/video generation | ~$30/tenant |

**6. Pre-computed Insights (saves 60-70% for common queries)**
Every 6 hours, a background job pre-computes common insights per tenant:
- Top performing channel
- Worst ROAS campaign
- Pipeline bottleneck
- Health score changes
- Anomaly detection

Store these as JSON. When user asks a common question, return pre-computed answer instead of calling LLM.

**7. Context Window Management**
Never send the full conversation history on every request. Summarize older messages into a compact context. Keep only the last 5 messages in full + a summary of prior context.

### Estimated AI Cost Per Tenant/Month

| Tenant Activity Level | Without Optimization | With All 7 Strategies |
|-----------------------|---------------------|----------------------|
| Low (10 queries/day) | ~$15/mo | ~$3/mo |
| Medium (30 queries/day) | ~$45/mo | ~$10/mo |
| High (100 queries/day) | ~$150/mo | ~$25/mo |

At 100 tenants (medium usage): **$1,000/mo vs $4,500/mo** — strategies save ~78%.

### Bedrock Setup Checklist

- [ ] AWS account with Bedrock access enabled
- [ ] Request model access: Claude Haiku, Claude Sonnet, Claude Opus
- [ ] Request model access: Titan Embeddings (for semantic cache)
- [ ] Request model access: Stable Diffusion XL (for image generation)
- [ ] Set up IAM role with Bedrock invoke permissions
- [ ] Configure provisioned throughput for Sonnet (most used tier)
- [ ] Set up CloudWatch alarms for cost monitoring

---

## Higgsfield AI Integration — Creative Generation

### What It Provides

Higgsfield is a **unified gateway to 100+ AI models** through one API:

| Capability | Models Available | Cost |
|-----------|-----------------|------|
| Text-to-Video | Sora 2, Veo 3.1, Kling 3.0, WAN 2.5 | ~$0.86/video |
| Image-to-Video | Same + Cinema Studio (virtual camera physics) | ~$0.86/video |
| Text-to-Image | FLUX, Seedream 4.5, Soul 2.0 | ~$0.12/image |
| Voice Cloning | Higgsfield Speak (multilingual, Spanish) | Per-use |
| Cinema Studio | 50+ cinematic camera presets (zooms, pans, drones) | Included |

### SDK (Node.js)

```typescript
import { createHiggsfieldClient } from 'higgsfield';
const client = createHiggsfieldClient('KEY_ID:KEY_SECRET');
```

Official repos: `higgsfield-ai/higgsfield-js` (Node), `higgsfield-ai/higgsfield-client` (Python)

### Pricing

| Plan | Price | Credits | API Access |
|------|-------|---------|-----------|
| Creator | $149/mo ($49.80 annual) | 6,000/mo | Yes |
| Enterprise | Custom | Custom | Yes + dedicated infra |
| Segmind (pay-per-use) | No subscription | ~$0.12/img, $0.86/vid | Yes |

**Recommendation:** Start with Segmind pay-per-use to avoid subscription overhead. Switch to Creator plan when volume justifies it (~1,200+ generations/month).

### Use Cases in NodeLabz

| Feature | What AI Generates | Trigger |
|---------|------------------|---------|
| Ad Creative Generator | Product photo → video ad with camera movement | User clicks "Generar Video" in campaign builder |
| Email Banner Generation | Text prompt → branded header image | AI chat: "genera un banner para mi campaña de verano" |
| Social Media Content | Product/service description → carousel images or reel | Automation workflow action node |
| WhatsApp Marketing | Short promo video for WhatsApp broadcast | Campaign creation flow |
| Report Cover | Custom branded cover for PDF reports | Report export |
| Landing Page Hero | AI-generated hero images for campaigns | (future — post Phase 1) |

### Cost Control for Media Generation

- Gate behind PROFESIONAL+ plans (not available on INICIO/CRECIMIENTO)
- Credit system: each tenant gets X generations/month based on plan
- Image generation first (cheaper at $0.12), video as premium ($0.86)
- Cache generated assets — reuse across campaigns
- Batch generation during off-peak hours for scheduled campaigns

### Setup Checklist

- [ ] Higgsfield account at cloud.higgsfield.ai
- [ ] API key (key ID + key secret)
- [ ] Install `higgsfield` npm package
- [ ] OR: Segmind account for pay-per-use access
- [ ] Set up generation credit tracking per tenant
- [ ] Add generation UI to campaign builder + email builder

## Post Phase 1 — What Comes Next

Phase 2 (Financial Layer) begins after 50 paying clients validate Phase 1:
- QuickBooks / Xero integration
- Deeper Stripe (invoices, subscriptions, MRR, churn)
- Cash flow analysis
- Cross-domain AI insights (marketing spend → revenue → profitability)

See `PRODUCT-VISION.md` for the full 4-phase expansion roadmap.
